t_ch: 今天的演出就到此为止，感谢你的捧场啾。
t_en: Today's show ends here. Thank you for coming everyone, tweet.

t_ch: 朋友，要来点饮料吗？啾啾。
t_en: My friend, how about a drink? Tweet tweet.

t_ch: 无论口味和风格，只要你能想到的，就没有我调不出来的啾。
t_en: Whatever taste or theme, if you can think of it, there's nothing that I can't mix, tweet.

t_ch: 一杯鸡翅味汽水。
t_en: A chicken-wing-flavored soda, please.

t_ch: 小鸟怎么会调这种饮料啊，你是来找茬啾？
t_en: How could a bird make that? Are you looking for trouble, tweet?

t_ch: 一杯汽油，加糖。
t_en: A glass of gasoline, extra sugar, please.

t_ch: 照这种方法调出来的饮料，会被其他客人举报的啾。
t_en: I'd be reported by other patrons if I made a drink with this ingredient, tweet.

t_ch: 我算看出来了，你就是故意来消遣本鸟的，根本没打算点单啾。
t_en: I can see it now. You're just here to make entertainment out of me and have no intention of placing an order, tweet.

t_ch: 小鸟很生气，小鸟要飞走了，再也不理你了啾。
t_en: I'm so upset! I'm going to ignore you and fly away now, tweet.

t_ch: 啾啾，没想到，居然有人能跟上我的舞步啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet, not bad, you're able to keep up with my dance moves, tweet.

t_ch: 怎样？有没有迷上我啾？一般人可没机会看到这样的表演啾。
t_en: How about it? Fallen for my charm yet, tweet? Most won't even get a chance to witness such a performance, tweet.

t_ch: 就看到三根毛在动。
t_en: All I see are three feather pinions moving.

t_ch: 真正的杰作，它的绝大部分都藏在水下，观众能看到的，就只有水上的三根羽毛啾。
t_en: A true work of art like this would have most of its parts hidden underwater, with the only thing visible to the audience being the three feather pinions reaching out, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，本鸟宽宏大量，不和你这不懂艺术的家伙计较啾。
t_en: Goodbye. I am far too magnanimous to debate with an uncultured swine like you, tweet.

t_ch: 今天的剧场好冷清啾，让我来活跃气氛啾。
t_en: The theater's so empty today, tweet. Lemme liven it up, tweet.

t_ch: 真是奇了怪了，不是说会有很多观众来美梦剧场吗？怎么只有我一个啾？
t_en: This is really strange, didn't they say there'd be a huge crowd coming to the Dreamgood Theater? Why am I the only one here, tweet?

t_ch: 他们迷路了。
t_en: They're lost.

t_ch: 这可太糟糕了，希望人没事啾。
t_en: That's terrible, I hope everyone's all right, tweet.

t_ch: 他们被抓了。
t_en: They've been captured.

t_ch: 原来他们都是坏人吗？哈努兄弟会好好收拾他们的啾。
t_en: So they're all baddies then? Brother Hanu will take real good care of them, tweet.

t_ch: 一个人都不来，我一只鸟待在这儿也太尴尬了，还是回大树好了啾。
t_en: No one's here at all. It's so awkward being the only lonesome birdie here. Better just head back to the Great Tree, tweet.

t_ch: 请留步啾，这里是副舞台，主角请走其他路线啾。
t_en: Hold on, tweet. This is the second stage. Main stars should use another route, tweet.

t_ch: 可别小瞧了我们，配角也是很重要的啾。
t_en: Don't underestimate us, supporting actors are very important too, tweet.

t_ch: 谢谢你们的表演。
t_en: Thank you for the show.

t_ch: 不客气啾，表演的成功属于每一位参与者啾。
t_en: Don't mention it, tweet. Every performer contributes to a show's success, tweet.

t_ch: 配角你神气什么？
t_en: What's a supporting actor like you strutting about for?

t_ch: 哼哼，喜欢折纸小鸟的人可多了，超乎你的想象啾。
t_en: Heh-heh, there are plenty of people — beyond your wildest imagination — who like origami birds, tweet.

t_ch: 再见啾，我要去鸟生的下一个舞台了，在那里，我可是主角啾。
t_en: Goodbye, tweet. I'm heading off to the next stage of life's soaring success. I'm going to play a lead role there, tweet.

t_ch: 锵锵，粉色胡子的大叔！是不是很好看啾？
t_en: Tadaa, a pink-bearded geezer! How do I look, tweet?

t_ch: 没想到啊，没想到，大叔你看着浓眉大眼，整的还蛮花哨啾。
t_en: Well well, you look quite fancy with your bushy brows and big eyes, tweet.

t_ch: 明明是你贴上去的吧！
t_en: You totally glued those on yourself!

t_ch: 哪、哪有！你不要凭空污小鸟的清白啾。
t_en: I—I did not! You shouldn't slander my innocence, tweet.

t_ch: 算了，不和你解释，小鸟要去找下一个倒霉蛋啾。
t_en: Just forget it, I'm done explaining to you. I'm off to look for another unlucky fool, tweet.

t_ch: 呼呼，贵宾区的沙发就是好，比自己搭的窝舒服多了啾。
t_en: Ho-ho, the couch in the VIP area is the best, it's so much more comfy than my own nest, tweet.

t_ch: 演出什么时候开场呢？我都快等不及了啾。
t_en: When does the show start? I can't wait, tweet.

t_ch: 你买票了吗？
t_en: You bought your ticket yet?

t_ch: 啾！当然买了票，我可是好梦剧场的常客啾。
t_en: Tweet! Of course I have, I'm a regular of Dreamgood Theater, tweet.

t_ch: 鸟也能当贵宾？
t_en: Can a bird also be a VIP guest?

t_ch: 小鸟可是钟表小子的好朋友，是贵宾中的贵宾啾！
t_en: I'm a good friend of Clockie's, so I'm the VIP of VIPs, tweet!

t_ch: 啾啾，我也要回去了，否则妃色夫人该着急了啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet, I have to go back too, or Lady Vermilion will be upset with me, tweet.

t_ch: 贵宾区的甜品可是剧场的一绝，你可千万别错过啾。
t_en: The snacks in the VIP area are the highlight of the theater. You absolutely can't miss out, tweet.

t_ch: 义弟啾！你怎么这么惨啾，大哥对不起你啾！
t_en: My brother, tweet! You poor thing, tweet! I wasn't around when you needed me the most, tweet!

t_ch: 啾！你这坏蛋，为什么要打死我的坐骑，你赔我一只啾！
t_en: Tweet! You savage, why did you take my mount's life? Pay me back, tweet!

t_ch: 发出一声困惑的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a puzzled *hmph*.

t_ch: 虽然它是只恐龙，经常做坏事，但它还是我的好义弟啾。
t_en: It may just be a dinosaur that does bad stuff, but it's still my dear sworn brother, tweet.

t_ch: 只要它在我身边，所有小鸟见了我都会吓得发抖啾。
t_en: As long as it was by my side, all the birds would quake in fear once they saw me, tweet.

t_ch: 别以为你是哈努兄弟，我就会怕你，我一点都不怕怕怕怕怕，再见啾。
t_en: You think I'm scared of you just because you're Brother Hanu? I'm not s—s—s—scared at all! Goodbye, tweet.

t_ch: 完全没有抓到羽毛的手感，仿佛这只折纸小鸟正处于另一个奇异的位面一般。
t_en: There's no tangible feeling of feathers. It's as if this origami bird is in another bizarre dimension.

t_ch: 想办法钻进电视机里看看？说不定能在动画的世界找到它。
t_en: You can try to dive into the TV for a look? Maybe you'll find it in the world of animations.

t_ch: 据说，集齐<color=#dbc291ff>所有的《小小哈努行动》</color>，就能获得进入电视机参与动画拍摄的资格啾。
t_en: Apparently, gathering all episodes of Hanu's Adventure qualifies one to enter the television and participate in the filming of the animation, tweet.

t_ch: 滴答～折纸小鸟为你报时啾。
t_en: Tick-tock! Origami bird's here to tell you the time, tweet.

t_ch: 现在是匹诺康尼时间，不知道多少时不知道多少分……
t_en: It's unknown hour, unknown minutes in Penacony time right now...

t_ch: 这报时有意义？
t_en: Is there even a point in telling the time like this?

t_ch: 啾啾，只有聪明的小鸟才能读懂我的时间啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet, only smart birdies would know how to tell my time, tweet.

t_ch: 不知道多少秒啾。
t_en: And unknown seconds, tweet.

t_ch: 恭喜你，已经学会抢答了啾。
t_en: Congrats, you know how to buzz for the answer first, tweet.

t_ch: 妃色夫人说过，不要把重要的事情告诉陌生人，时间就很重要啾。
t_en: Lady Vermilion said not to mention anything important to strangers. Time is very important, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，本小鸟要去一个不知道的地方，去做不知道的事情啾。
t_en: Goodbye, I'm going to an unknown place, to do something unknown, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿，看到那片混乱的中心了吗？有好多东西都被吸进去了啾。
t_en: Hey, see that chaotic center? A lot of great loot has been sucked into it, tweet.

t_ch: 这么危险的地方，里面肯定藏着木头老爷的宝藏啾。
t_en: Old Man Wood's treasure must be hidden in such a dangerous place, tweet.

t_ch: 什么好东西？
t_en: What kinda loot?

t_ch: 不知道，木头老爷的宝贝，都是些看不懂的树叶啾。
t_en: No idea, Old Man Wood's treasures are just some weird leaves, tweet.

t_ch: 木头老爷？
t_en: Old Man Wood?

t_ch: 乱蓬蓬的树叶头发，总是笑眯眯地，那就是木头老爷啾。
t_en: With that head of unkempt, leafy hair, and that grin on his face — that's Old Man Wood, tweet.

t_ch: 啾，本来打算叫上你一起，但你好像不会飞啾。
t_en: Tweet, I was going to get you to come too, but I don't think you can fly, tweet.

t_ch: 我还是回大树去找几个帮手吧，让我们去冒险啾！
t_en: I'd better head back to the Great Tree to look for some help so we can go on an adventure, tweet!

t_ch: 啾！朋友，快来帮帮忙，这里有人打起来了。
t_en: Tweet! Lend me a hand, friend. Someone's fighting over here.

t_ch: 你们不要再打了，小鸟把羽毛给你们，大家一起做好朋友啾。
t_en: Stop fighting! Here's the feather, let's all be friends, tweet.

t_ch: 人在哪儿？
t_en: Where's everyone?

t_ch: 就在镜子里面，你看，根本没有停手的迹象啾。
t_en: In that mirror. Look, they aren't showing signs of stopping at all, tweet.

t_ch: 打架？算我一个。
t_en: A fight? Count me in.

t_ch: 哎呀，现场已经够乱了，你就别火上浇油了啾。
t_en: Ahh, this is messy enough, stop adding fuel to the fire, tweet.

t_ch: 一个个都是笨蛋家伙，完全不听小鸟的劝，再打就要出人命了啾。
t_en: You're all doodoo-heads who don't listen to me. Someone's going to get offed at this rate, tweet.

t_ch: 对了，我去找妃色夫人，它肯定有办法治住这些人啾。
t_en: Oh right, I have to go get Lady Vermilion, she must have a way to stop these people, tweet.

t_ch: 啾，朋友，你也被这天才的设计震慑住了吗？
t_en: Tweet, have you been dumbfounded by this genius' design?

t_ch: 看那整齐划一的石头，那精美绝伦的雕刻，啊，我已经爱上这里了啾。
t_en: Look at that immaculate stone, that exquisitely elegant sculpture. Ah, I'm in love with this place, tweet.

t_ch: 你设计的？
t_en: You designed it?

t_ch: 多么美丽的误会啾，倘若这里真是我搭建的，小鸟便死而无憾了啾。
t_en: What a beautiful misunderstanding, tweet. I'd die with no regrets if I was the one who built this place, tweet.

t_ch: 我也很喜欢。
t_en: I like it very much too.

t_ch: 这就是艺术，无论是人类还是小鸟都能为之感动啾。
t_en: This is art capable of moving both humans and birds, tweet.

t_ch: 与它相比，我的作品是那样黯淡，我的学识是那样浅薄啾。
t_en: My works are much more dismal than it, and my knowledge much shallower, tweet.

t_ch: 再见啾，朋友，我要去精进自己的技艺，总有一天，我会搭建出比这更厉害的作品啾。
t_en: Goodbye, tweet. I have to hone my own craft, friend. One day, I will make something that trumps this masterpiece, tweet.

t_ch: 啾啾，啾？我怎么跑气球里了？
t_en: Tweet tweet, tweet? How'd I get in this balloon?

t_ch: 这个房间好奇怪，每次有人进来，它都会「刷」地一下变成新的样子啾。
t_en: This room is really strange. Each time someone enters, it will rearrange its layout with a "swoosh," tweet.

t_ch: 刚刚我还在天上，周围突然转转转了一下，我就到这里来了啾。
t_en: I was just minding my own business in the sky, and then the surroundings suddenly started to spin round and round, and now here I am, tweet.

t_ch: 搞得我现在还晕乎乎的啾。
t_en: I'm so dizzy, tweet.

t_ch: 趁着它现在还没变，要赶紧飞出去，不然又该迷路了啾。
t_en: Gotta fly outta here before it changes, otherwise I'm gonna lose my way again, tweet.

t_ch: 好心人，好心人，你会弹钢琴吗？啾啾？
t_en: Oh, kind-hearted stranger, can you play the piano? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 无论我怎么折腾，这台钢琴就是发不出声音啾。
t_en: This piano won't make a sound no matter how much I bang on it, tweet.

t_ch: 因为在镜子里吧…
t_en: Probably because it's in the mirror...

t_ch: 呜呜呜，为什么我不是镜子小鸟，而是折纸小鸟啾。
t_en: *wail* Why am I an origami bird, and not a mirror bird, tweet.

t_ch: 说明你技术太差。
t_en: Skill issue.

t_ch: 小鸟的爪子果然不适合弹琴吗？下次我用翅膀试试啾。
t_en: A bird's claws aren't suited to playing the piano after all? I'll use my wings next time, tweet.

t_ch: 妃色夫人说，懂得一门乐器的小鸟能更受欢迎，可我什么都不会啾。
t_en: Lady Vermilion said that birdies who can play an instrument are much more popular, but I just can't seem to be able to pick any up, tweet.

t_ch: 不行，不能气馁，我要找到一台小鸟也能用的乐器啾！
t_en: No, I can't give up. I must find an instrument that even a birdie can play, tweet!

t_ch: 啾！气球可以打，发型可以乱，但别动我的镜子啾！
t_en: Tweet! You can pop balloons, hairstyles can be tussled, but no one touches my mirror, tweet!

t_ch: 看看镜子里的本鸟，尤其是那三根靓丽的头发，连我自己都不禁心动啾。
t_en: Just look at me in the mirror, especially those three magnificent feather pinions. I can't help but be moved by my own reflection, tweet.

t_ch: 镜子镜子，我是不是最漂亮的小鸟啾？
t_en: Mirror mirror answer me, am I the fairest birdie, tweet?

t_ch: 我才是！
t_en: I am!

t_ch: 你要爪子没爪子，要翅膀没翅膀，哪里像小鸟了啾？！
t_en: You've got no talons and no wings. How're you even a bird, tweet!?

t_ch: 你当然是。
t_en: Of course you are.

t_ch: 呼呼呼，聪明的镜子，还有聪明的人类啾。
t_en: Ho-ho, smart mirror, and smart human, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，本鸟要把这绝世的美貌带给更多人，让他们都饱饱眼福啾。
t_en: Goodbye. I have to share my gorgeous self with more people and treat them to a feast for the eyes, tweet.

t_ch: 这是贝洛伯格最常见的铁栏，因室外的低温而蒙上了一层白霜。
t_en: This kind of iron fencing is seen all over Belobog. It is coated with a layer of white frost due to the low temperatures.

t_ch: 它看上去...非常地诱人，就像抹着糖霜的蛋糕一样。
t_en: It looks... extremely tempting, like a cake covered in sweet frosting.

t_ch: 你不禁开始好奇：它会是甜的吗？
t_en: Your curious mind begins to wander: Is it sweet?

t_ch: 舔一口。
t_en: Lick it.

t_ch: 还是算了。
t_en: I'll pass.

t_ch: 你止住了这个疯狂的想法——什么人会去舔栏杆啊？
t_en: You managed to restrain your crazy thoughts. What would possess someone to lick a fence?

t_ch: 秉持着「开拓」的精神，你对冻栏杆伸出了舌头......
t_en: Sticking to your experimental spirit as a "Trailblazer," you stretch your tongue out toward the frozen railing...

t_ch: 前段，甜。中段…嗯，还是甜，略涩。后段——
t_en: The first taste is sweet. The middle part... hmm, it's still sweet, but a little tart, too. And at the end —

t_ch: ——没有后段，因为你绵软的舌头已经与冰冷的栏杆亲密交融，不分彼此了。
t_en: — There is no end, because your tongue and the railing have already formed an intimate bond, never to be separated.

t_ch: 你的舌头被粘得十分牢固，栅栏本身也巍然不动。试图用蛮力脱困——想都别想，除非你已经下定决心这辈子都不再说话了，不然那绝对是自讨苦吃。
t_en: Your tongue is stuck fast, and the fence remains immovable. Don't even think about fixing this situation with brute force, unless you've already set your heart on never speaking again. That is just asking for trouble.

t_ch: 你被困住了。万万没想到，战胜过无数外宇宙之冰的勇猛开拓者居然会被一根结霜的栏杆击败…但不要紧，这造物之柱周围有很多熟人，总有人能救你于危难中。
t_en: You're trapped. Who could have ever thought that the brave Trailblazer that defeated countless Ice out of Space would be defeated by an icy fence... But, don't panic, there are lots of people around who know you from the Pillars of Creation project. Someone will come and save you in your time of need.

t_ch: 那么，你打算向谁求助呢？
t_en: So, will you call for help?

t_ch: 虎克，救我啊！
t_en: Hook, help me!

t_ch: 嗯？刚才是不是听见了荣誉队员的声音？
t_en: Hm? Wasn't that the honorary member's voice?

t_ch: 啊，荣誉队员怎么变成这个样子了？！
t_en: Ah, how did you end up like this, honorary member!?

t_ch: 别怕，「漆黑的虎克大人」这就救你出来！
t_en: Don't worry, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great will rescue you!

t_ch: 伴随着洞洞机开启的轰鸣声，你完整分离了舌头和栏杆——倘若把你换成其他人，这两者可能都要不保。
t_en: With the roar of the Diggertron, you managed to perfectly separate your tongue and the fence. If this had been anyone else, both may have been lost.

t_ch: 不用谢！鼹鼠党的队员就是要互相帮助！
t_en: You're welcome! We Moles members must always help each other!

t_ch: 我还没说谢谢呢...
t_en: I didn't say thank you yet...

t_ch: 希儿，你在吗？
t_en: Seele, are you there?

t_ch: 你的声音在空气中徜徉，却没有得到回应。
t_en: Your voice lingers on the air, but a response never comes.

t_ch: 你猜这会儿她应该在下层区的某处惩奸除恶，没空搭理一个舌头黏在栏杆上的可怜开拓者。
t_en: You imagine that she must be in the Underworld punishing the wicked. She has no time to come and rescue a pitiful Trailblazer that has their tongue stuck to a fence.

t_ch: 看来得找其他人了......
t_en: Looks like you'll have to call someone else...

t_ch: （向一旁的铁卫寻求帮助）
t_en: (Call for help from the nearby Silvermane Guard)
This...

t_ch: ....您体验风土人情的方式还真是…奇特。稍等，我这就来帮您……
t_en: ...Your way of experiencing the local views is certainly... unique. Wait a moment, I'll help you...

t_ch: 铁卫小心翼翼地帮你分离了舌头和栏杆。不难想象，今天的趣闻很快就会在部队内部传开。
t_en: The Silvermane Guard carefully works to separate your tongue from the fence. You can already imagine news of this story spreading through the ranks like wildfire.

t_ch: 之后有必要在这立个牌子...
t_en: We'll have to put a sign here in the future...

t_ch: ....噗...您说得对，我们这就去办。
t_en: ...*snickers*... Quite right. We will do that immediately.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生，我是变量！
t_en: Mr. Svarog, I'm the variable!

t_ch: 史瓦罗没有出现。
t_en: Svarog does not appear.

t_ch: 还是想想其他人吧......
t_en: You'll have to think of someone else...

t_ch: 想想其他人。
t_en: Think of someone else.

t_ch: 帮帮我，克拉拉！
t_en: Help me, Clara!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}？！你怎么......
t_en: {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}!? How did you...

t_ch: 请等一下，我这就去找帕金斯来帮忙。
t_en: Wait a moment. I'll get Perkins to come and help.

t_ch: 一台机兵从工地上赶来，帮你分离了舌头和栏杆，这一过程吸引了不少人围观喝彩......
t_en: An automaton comes rushing over from the construction site to help you separate your tongue from the pole. This whole process brought applause and admiration from a lot of onlookers...

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}还有没有哪里不舒服？
t_en: {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, do you feel okay now?

t_ch: 没有了，谢谢克拉拉。
t_en: I'm okay. Thank you, Clara.

t_ch: 没关系的，克拉拉也想帮上大家。
t_en: No problem. I want to help everyone, too!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}之后也多加小心啊。我们的身体毕竟不是机器零件，要多加保护才行。
t_en: You be careful from now on, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}. Our bodies aren't made of mechanical parts. We need to protect them properly.

t_ch: 娜塔莎医生！
t_en: Dr. Natasha!

t_ch: 怎么这么不小心，让我看看...
t_en: How could you be so careless? Let me take a look...

t_ch: 在短暂且专业的处理后，你的舌头和栏杆分离了。但娜塔莎看向你的眼神却变得复杂起来。
t_en: After a brief and professional treatment, your tongue is separated from the fence. However, the look in Natasha's eyes has grown a little complicated.

t_ch: 几年前，我也为虎克他们处理过这种情况...小孩子就是喜欢胡闹。
t_en: A few years ago, I had to help Hook out of a situation like this, too... Children will always cause trouble, I guess.

t_ch: 后续要是还不舒服的话，就抹上这个药吧。
t_en: If you feel any pain later, use this balm.

t_ch: 谢谢娜塔莎医生！
t_en: Thank you, Dr. Natasha!

t_ch: 下次小心就好。如果你再被困在哪座冰山上，我可帮不了你。
t_en: Just be careful next time. If it's a snowy mountain you end up getting stuck on, I can't help you.

t_ch: 玲可，求求你！
t_en: Lynx, help me, please!
Ah, you...

t_ch: 稍等，我帮你处理一下。
t_en: Wait a minute. I'll take care of this.

t_ch: 玲可取出自己的热水瓶，开始帮你脱困。你的舌头从冰冷变得滚烫，直到最后才与栏杆分离。
t_en: Lynx pulls out her hot water flask and begins to help you. Your tongue goes from freezing cold to scalding hot, until it finally breaks free from the fence.

t_ch: 这样的事在极地经常发生，因为舌头上的水会迅速结冰，之后就牢牢粘住了。
t_en: These things happen a lot in arctic areas, because the saliva on the tongue freezes quickly and sticks tight.

t_ch: 下次出门记得带个暖水瓶，再遇到这种情况就有办法了。
t_en: Remember to bring warm water with you next time you go out, then you can deal with this yourself if this happens again.

t_ch: 你好熟练...
t_en: You really know your stuff...

t_ch: ...姐姐经常说，不许舔栏杆，不许舔栏杆…我倒是想问，哪个正常人会去舔啊？
t_en: ...My sister often says: "Don't lick fence posts. Don't lick fence posts..." I'm more curious about why would anyone want to do that.

t_ch: 向随便哪个星神祈祷。
t_en: Pray to a random Aeon.

t_ch: 没有星神回应你。
t_en: No Aeon responds.

t_ch: 回想之前的人。
t_en: Think back to someone from before.

t_ch: 舌头上的冻感消失了，取而代之的是全身火辣辣的疼痛。「小孩子下手真是不知轻重啊」——你这样想着。
t_en: The freezing sensation on your tongue disappears, but is replaced with a burning sensation throughout your body. You think to yourself: "Kids really don't know how to hold back!"

t_ch: 如果娜塔莎在身边的话，她一定会建议你赶紧去找个界域定锚。
t_en: If Natasha were here, you know she'd suggest you find a Space Anchor immediately.

t_ch: 他忍住了笑声——你打心底里对他的矜持表示感激。
t_en: He restrains his laughter, for which you express sincere gratitude.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}？你这是在做什么？
t_en: {NICKNAME}? What are you doing?

t_ch: 虽然表情依旧淡定，但她明显是在嘲讽你的幼稚行为。
t_en: Though her expression is as calm as ever, she is clearly mocking you for your childish actions.

t_ch: 「活该」——玲可没有再言语，但你确信自己听到了那两个字。
t_en: "Serves you right"! ...Though Lynx didn't actually say anything, you are certain you heard those words.

t_ch: 想靠这种小伎俩骗取阿哈的瞥视？哈！你还是太嫩了。
t_en: You wish to use such pitiful tricks to steal Aha's gaze? How naive.

t_ch: 这可能是好事。你又仔细想了想——史瓦罗会用何种方式帮助你解脱实在是个不可预知的谜团。
t_en: This is probably for the best. Thinking about it, the method that Svarog would use to free you is an unpredictable mystery.

t_ch: 为了您的安全，请勿舔舐栏杆。
t_en: For your own safety, do not lick this fence.

t_ch: 千万别掉下去…
t_en: Please don't fall off...

t_ch: 别打镜子啾！
t_en: Don't smash the mirror, tweet!

t_ch: 看不到我啾。
t_en: Can't see me, tweet.

t_ch: 啾！你好，要不要握个爪啾？
t_en: Tweet! Hello, want to shake claws, tweet?

t_ch: 我在大树里可是鸟气很高的，想和我握爪的小鸟能排满美梦小镇啾。
t_en: I'm super popular at the Great Tree. Birdies who want to shake claws with me can fill up the entire Dreamville, tweet.

t_ch: 怎么了，好像很抗拒的样子——莫非你是个害羞的人啾？
t_en: What, you look put off... Are you too shy, tweet?

t_ch: 感觉脏脏的…
t_en: Feels dirty...

t_ch: 哼哼…看来，世上也是有能抵挡住在下魅力的人啾。
t_en: Heh-heh... Looks like there are those who can withstand my charm, tweet.

t_ch: （试着握爪）
t_en: (Try shaking its claw)

t_ch: 哎呀，差点忘了！折纸的爪子太小，没法握爪啾！
t_en: Doh, I almost forgot! Origami claws are too small to be shaken, tweet!

t_ch: 再见，矜持的朋友，就当我在心里和你握爪了啾。
t_en: Goodbye, reserved friend. We'll just say we shook claws in our hearts, tweet.

t_ch: （找到所有折纸小鸟了，回「学堂」跟猫头鹰老师汇报吧...）
t_en: (I found all the Origami Birds. Time to report back to Professor Owl at the school...)

t_ch: 呜呜呜，为什么要把我找出来……
t_en: Boohoo, why did you find me...

t_ch: 我是一只没用的小鸟，连自己的巢都搭不好，只配住在垃圾袋里啾……
t_en: I'm a useless birdie that can't even build its own nest. I'm only fit to live in a trash bag, tweet...

t_ch: 这垃圾袋归我了！
t_en: This trash bag is mine!

t_ch: 呜呜，居然连自己的巢都守不住…我真是最最没用的小鸟了啾……
t_en: Boohoo, I couldn't even defend my own nest... I'm really the most useless birdie alive, tweet...

t_ch: 总能学会的。
t_en: You'll learn eventually.

t_ch: 我知道自己是只无能的小鸟…但还是谢谢你的安慰啾。
t_en: I know I'm an inept bird... but I appreciate your comforting me, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，陌生人…这个垃圾袋就让给你了啾。
t_en: Goodbye, stranger... I'll give you this trash bag, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿！有没有被我的超级无敌霹雳闪光晃到眼睛啾？
t_en: Hey! Did I blind you with my super awesome radiance spark, tweet?

t_ch: 小鸟的羽毛能不能让灯光变得更亮，我正在研究这个问题啾。
t_en: Can a bird's feathers make light brighter? I'm researching this problem right now, tweet.

t_ch: 能感受到光线有什么不同吗？啾？
t_en: You feel the difference in the light? Tweet?

t_ch: 感觉没变化。
t_en: Not much.

t_ch: 这样啊，我下次换一种类型的灯泡再试试啾。
t_en: I see. I'll switch up the lightbulb and try again next time, tweet.

t_ch: 你出来后是暗了点。
t_en: It was darker after you left.

t_ch: 哼哼，我和那些只会搭巢的小鸟可不一样啾。
t_en: Heh-heh, I'm different from those birdies that only know how to build nests, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，朋友，总有一天，我会让清醒村从黑漆漆变成亮堂堂的啾！
t_en: Goodbye, friend. One day, I'll turn Rouseville's pitch-black darkness into a light show, tweet!

t_ch: 年轻的勇者，你终于来了啾。
t_en: You've finally come, young warrior, tweet.

t_ch: 看到这张地图了吗？本贤者为你标记出了宝藏的所在啾。
t_en: See this map? I've marked the location of a treasure for you, tweet.

t_ch: 猜猜你会在上面看到什么？两座巨大的——好险，差点就剧透了啾。
t_en: Guess what you can see on it? Two giant... That was close, I almost gave the answer away, tweet.

t_ch: 我的台词已经说完了，勇者，祝你好运啾。
t_en: I'm done with my lines. Good luck, warrior, tweet.

t_ch: 砰！有没有被吓到啾？
t_en: Boo! Did that scare you, tweet?

t_ch: 所有的小鸟都被超大的钟表小子吃掉了，猜猜为什么我还在啾？
t_en: All the birdies were eaten by the gigantic Clockie. Guess why I'm still here, tweet?

t_ch: 你是幽灵？
t_en: You're a ghost?

t_ch: 你会隐身？
t_en: You went invisible?

t_ch: 卟卟，猜错了啾，因为钟表小子不吃小鸟，我是骗你的啾。
t_en: Boo, wrong guess, tweet. It's because Clockie doesn't eat birds. I was just pulling your leg, tweet.

t_ch: 你的表现可真好玩，再见，下次还来逗你啾。
t_en: Your reaction was really fun. Goodbye, I'll come back next time to make you laugh, tweet.

t_ch: 你好啾，路过的朋友，能否解答我的一个疑问？
t_en: Hello, tweet. Passing friend, can you solve a riddle of mine?

t_ch: 为何音符能和钟表成为朋友，鸟儿却不能爱上一只猫？
t_en: Why can notes and clocks become friends, but birds can't fall in love with a cat?

t_ch: 猫和鸟是天敌。
t_en: Cats and birds are natural enemies.

t_ch: 连你也这么说，多么残酷的因果啾。
t_en: Even you say that... What a cruel karma, tweet.

t_ch: 玩得还挺大？
t_en: You're taking great risks.

t_ch: 哎，像你这样的普通人，怎会理解小鸟的爱恋啾。
t_en: *sigh* How would an ordinary person like you understand the love of birds, tweet.

t_ch: 猫啊，你为什么是猫呢？如果你是一只折纸小鸟该多好……
t_en: O cat, cat, wherefore art thou a cat? It'd be much better if you were an origami bird...

t_ch: 啾啾…永别了，我深爱的小猫，愿我离去后，你再也不会哭泣……
t_en: Tweet tweet... Farewell forever, my feline love. I hope you'll never cry once I leave...

t_ch: 哇！是新的客人，去下个站的话，要到我这儿买票啾。
t_en: Woah! A new guest. You need to get tickets from me if you're getting to the next stop, tweet.

t_ch: 让我看看，售票装置售票装置……
t_en: Let's see, ticketing device, where's my ticketing device...

t_ch: 啾！好像、好像坏掉了啾。
t_en: Tweet! I—It looks broken, tweet.

t_ch: 你可以假装没看见。
t_en: You can pretend not to have seen anything.

t_ch: 那我在这里卖票，岂不是毫无意义啾？
t_en: Wouldn't it be pointless for me to sell tickets here, then, tweet?

t_ch: 我都过去好几回了。
t_en: I've already been there a couple times.

t_ch: 呜呜呜，我真是只不称职的小鸟啾。
t_en: *sniffle* I'm a truly incompetent birdie, tweet.

t_ch: 但转过来想，把小鸟的青春浪费在这种地方，实在是不值啾。
t_en: But on second thought, wasting a birdie's youth on this place simply isn't worth it, tweet.

t_ch: 我记得站牌先生那里还有票，你去问问它好了——折纸小鸟，堂堂下班！现在就溜回大树睡懒觉啾！
t_en: I remember Mr. Signpost has a ticket. You best ask them. Origami bird, punching out! Time to slip back to the Great Tree and take a nap!

t_ch: 唔，桑拿时间结束了吗？啾啾？
t_en: Oh, is sauna time over? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 热腾腾的水蒸汽，就连羽毛也痛快得舒展起来了啾。
t_en: Even feathers will be loosened up by the hot steam, tweet.

t_ch: 不过，总感觉身子有点软趴趴的…啾……
t_en: But, I feel my body going woozy... Tweet...

t_ch: 翅膀打湿了吧？
t_en: Wings gotten wet?

t_ch: 纸也能蒸桑拿？
t_en: Can paper even be soaked in saunas?

t_ch: 折纸小鸟又不是真的纸，怎么会打湿呢…应该不会吧？啾？
t_en: Origami birds aren't made from actual paper, why would we get wet... Or would we? Tweet?

t_ch: 我、我现在就去找湛蓝爵士问问啾！
t_en: I... I'm going to look for Dr. Blues right now for answers, tweet!

t_ch: 啾！你是怎么识破我天衣无缝的伪装的？
t_en: Tweet! How did you see through my impeccable disguise?

t_ch: 为了赢过其他小鸟，我甚至特地找了个有头发的气球啾。
t_en: I even got a balloon with a wig to beat the other birdies, tweet.

t_ch: 你们发色都不一样。
t_en: Your hair color is different.

t_ch: 啾～这已经是最接近的颜色了。
t_en: Tweet! This was the most similar shade they had.

t_ch: 因为我火眼金睛。
t_en: Because my eyes see all.

t_ch: 咕，没想到自己居然输得这么简单。
t_en: Coo, I didn't think I'd lose so easily.

t_ch: 再见，我要回去看看，自己是不是最后一个被找到的啾。
t_en: Goodbye, I'm going to return and see if I'm the last birdie found, tweet.

t_ch: 恭喜，你是第一个发现本鸟作品的人啾！
t_en: Congrats, you're the first one who's discovered my work, tweet!

t_ch: 本鸟亲自上阵，给猫猫添上的三根头发，是不是很漂亮啾？
t_en: I personally went ahead and added three strands of hair to the cat. Beautiful, isn't it, tweet?

t_ch: 不太好看。
t_en: Not really.

t_ch: 画蛇添足了。
t_en: It was unnecessary.

t_ch: 怎么会…这可是我最满意的设计，原本打算给美梦小镇的每只猫都来一个的啾。
t_en: How so... I'm most pleased with this design. I was going to do every cat in Dreamville initially, tweet.

t_ch: 肯定是你不懂本鸟的艺术，我这就回去找其他小鸟评评理啾！
t_en: You must not understand my artistic flair. I'm going back to get the opinion of other birdies, tweet!

t_ch: 啾，钟表小子，你终于回来——什么呀，是个不认识的新面孔，害我白高兴一场啾。
t_en: Tweet, Clockie, you're finally back... Huh? An unfamiliar face. You got me all enthused for nothing, tweet.

t_ch: 那家伙，说着要带小镇上的朋友们一起去冒险，结果自己居然迟到了啾。
t_en: That guy said he was going to take all the town's friends along for an adventure, and yet he's running late, tweet.

t_ch: 看到那个老爷爷了吗？总感觉他的身上有股熟悉的味道啾。
t_en: See that old man over there? There's some familiar smell about him, tweet.

t_ch: 钟表小子说，他的朋友会开来一辆很大很大的车，能把美梦小镇的大家全都装进去，我猜呀，这个老爷爷肯定就是那位列车长啾。
t_en: Clockie said his friend would be driving a huge car here that fits everyone in Dreamville. My guess is this old man is that conductor, tweet.

t_ch: 糟了！突然想起来，我还有行李没有带，希望现在飞回大树还来得及啾！
t_en: Oh no! I suddenly remembered that I left some luggage at home. I hope it's not too late to fly back to the Great Tree, tweet!

t_ch: 你不懂，猫头鹰老师教育我们，事事都要尽力而为啾。
t_en: You won't understand — Professor Owl taught us to put in our utmost effort at everything, tweet.

t_ch: 不过垃圾桶待久了是有点味儿，回大树以后老被湛蓝爵士念叨啾。
t_en: But I do smell a bit after spending too much time in the trashcan. I was reprimanded by Dr. Blues when I went back to the Great Tree, tweet.

t_ch: 我要找一个比垃圾桶更好的藏身点，成为捉迷藏之王啾。
t_en: I'm going to look for a hiding spot that's better than a trashcan, and become the champion of Hide-and-Seek, tweet.

t_ch: 啾，朋友，你认识这个奶奶吗？
t_en: Tweet, you know this granny, friend?

t_ch: 她望着前面，一会儿笑嘻嘻，一会儿哭唧唧的，好吓鸟啾。
t_en: She's just staring ahead, laughing one moment, then weeping the next. Scary, tweet.

t_ch: 心里难过吧。
t_en: She's probably sad.

t_ch: 心里有坏东西憋着可不好啾，会飞不起来的。
t_en: It's not good to keep negative thoughts in. You won't be able to fly.

t_ch: 睹物思人吧。
t_en: She's probably thinking of someone.

t_ch: 如果想念一个人，为什么不直接面对面说话啾？
t_en: If she misses someone, why doesn't she just talk to that person face-to-face, tweet?

t_ch: 人类真的好难懂，要是大家天天都笑嘻嘻的就好了啾。
t_en: Humans are so hard to understand. It would be great if everyone was happy every day, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，我要回大树了——希望你和老奶奶也能开心，啾啾。
t_en: Goodbye, I'm returning to the Great Tree. I wish you and the granny happiness too, tweet tweet.

t_ch: 嘿，你轻点，万一把热气球戳破了，我还怎么降落啾。
t_en: Hey, be gentle. How am I going to descend if you pop the hot-air balloon, tweet?

t_ch: 看看这个高度，要是我从这儿跳下去，肯定非常刺激啾。
t_en: Just look at this height. It must be thrilling to jump down from here, tweet.

t_ch: 你不是会飞吗？
t_en: Don't you know how to fly?

t_ch: 对哦，我怎么没有想到这点啾？
t_en: Oh right. Why didn't I think of that, tweet?

t_ch: 这气球靠谱吗？
t_en: You sure this balloon can hold up?

t_ch: 虽然比不上翅膀，但也聊胜于无——等等，我是不是有翅膀啾？
t_en: Can't compare to wings for sure, but it's better than nothing... Hold on, do I have wings, tweet?

t_ch: 多谢你的提醒，朋友…一想到自己会飞，这儿好像也没那么刺激了啾。
t_en: Thank you for your reminder, my friend... Suddenly it doesn't sound so thrilling when I realize I can fly, tweet.

t_ch: 再见，让我们在大树再会吧——能不能让自己忘掉飞行呢啾……
t_en: Goodbye, let's meet again at the Great Tree. I wonder if I can forget how to fly, tweet...

t_ch: 啾！我是邪恶的海盗大统领，比石头老板还要恐怖啾。
t_en: Tweet! I'm the evil pirate captain! More menacing than Boss Stone, tweet.

t_ch: 我的铁皮战舰坚不可摧，就连炸弹头也没法打破它啾。
t_en: My ironhide cruiser is impenetrable. Not even Bombhead can pierce it, tweet.

t_ch: 吃我一棒！
t_en: Say hello to my baseball bat!

t_ch: 棒下留鸟，邪恶的海盗大统领向你投降啾。
t_en: Spare the bat! This evil pirate captain yields to you, tweet.

t_ch: 哇，好厉害。
t_en: Wow, that's impressive.

t_ch: 哼，看你这么尊敬本统领，就封你个海盗王当当啾。
t_en: Hmph, I see that you respect me so much, I'll bestow you the title of Pirate King, tweet.

t_ch: 本统领又要踏上新的征程了，愿我们来日再见啾。
t_en: Yours truly is about to embark on a new journey. May we meet again in the future, tweet.

t_ch: 您好！真幸运，能与您共享这片美景啾。
t_en: Hello! How fortunate to be able to share this beautiful scenery with you, tweet.

t_ch: 看到那两个钟表小子了吗？这可是小鸟们辛辛苦苦搭建出来的啾。
t_en: Do you see those two Clockies? That's the result of the hard work of the birdies, tweet.

t_ch: 真是个神气的家伙啾，也不知道他什么时候才能回来。
t_en: He's quite something, tweet. Not sure when he'll be back though.

t_ch: 啾，看到远处的花园了吗？这可是湛蓝爵士最得意的设计。
t_en: Hey, see the distant garden? That's Dr. Blues' best design.

t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 等到熄灯后，我的人偶会带着我环游校园啾！
t_en: After the lights go out, my puppets will bring me on a tour around the school, tweet!

t_ch: 但现在被你发现了，人偶就不能动了啾。
t_en: But since you've discovered them, the puppets cannot move anymore, tweet.

t_ch: 讨厌你，我要再去找个坐骑了啾——
t_en: I hate you. Now I have to go find another mount, tweet...

t_ch: 小鸟坐热气球，上面空气很好啾。
t_en: Sitting in a hot air balloon... I find the air above so great, tweet.

t_ch: 沐浴聚光灯，我也会像大鸟一样金闪闪的啾。
t_en: Bathing under the spotlight... I will shine brightly like the big birds, tweet.

t_ch: 可惜你把我的气球打破了啾……
t_en: Sadly, you burst my balloon, tweet...

t_ch: 不好意思。
t_en: Sorry.

t_ch: 我只是随手按了下攻击。
t_en: I just accidentally pressed the attack button.

t_ch: 哎，没关系，我再去找个新的吧啾。
t_en: *sigh* Never mind. I'll just find a new one, tweet.

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜，轻点，轻点啾！
t_en: Woah woah woah, gently, gently, tweet!

t_ch: 这才不是猫头鹰老师的羽毛，他是大秃头，大秃头啾。
t_en: These aren't Professor Owl's feathers. He's a big baldie, a baldie, tweet.

t_ch: 掌管才能的神看了猫头鹰老师一眼，揪走他三根羽毛，又赐予他知识。
t_en: The god of talent glanced at Professor Owl, plucked out three of his feathers, and granted him knowledge.

t_ch: 秃头，秃头，是知识的诅咒——难怪我算术老是不及格啾。
t_en: Baldie, baldie, it's a curse of knowledge — no wonder I keep failing in math, tweet.

t_ch: 我也可以赐予你知识。
t_en: I can also grant you knowledge.

t_ch: 不要，我还是做只漂亮的小笨鸟吧。啾~
t_en: No, I'd rather be a beautiful dumb bird. Tweet.

t_ch: 所以你是笨蛋吗？
t_en: So you're dumb?

t_ch: 说什么呢，我是聪明与美丽并存的小鸟啾！
t_en: What are you saying? I am the epitome of a bird with both knowledge and beauty, tweet!

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师上课…上课…上课，折纸小鸟划水…划水…划水啾。
t_en: Professor Owl lectures... Lectures... Lectures. Origami birds swimming... Swimming... Swimming, tweet.

t_ch: 你是纸做的，不能泡水。
t_en: You're made from paper. You can't soak in water.

t_ch: 听不见，听不见啾……
t_en: Can't hear you, can't hear you...

t_ch: 不准在水池里游泳！
t_en: You can't swim in the decorative pool!

t_ch: 你说的对……
t_en: You're right...

t_ch: 所以我要买下这所学堂！新规定——从今以后水池可以游泳啾。
t_en: That's why I'm going to buy school! New rule — from today on, all pools can be swum in, tweet!

t_ch: 划水…划水…划水……
t_en: Swimming... swimming... swimming...

t_ch: 啾——翅膀被水泡烂啦！要吹干吹干啾——
t_en: Tweet!!! My wings are waterlogged! Time to dry them, dry them, tweet...

t_ch: 您好您好，请跟着箭头走啾~
t_en: Hello hello, please follow the arrows, tweet!

t_ch: 筑梦师小鸟为了动线设计操碎了心，太简单怕无聊，太复杂怕找不准路。
t_en: The Dreamweaver bird tore its feathers out over the route design: If it's too simple, it might be boring. If it's too complicated, people might get lost.

t_ch: 所以安排了这些箭头，还有我，给你们引路啾。
t_en: So these arrows are arranged and there's me, who will guide you along, tweet.

t_ch: 破事儿真多，弄个小地图不就好了啾！
t_en: So many problems! A minimap would have solved the problem, tweet.

t_ch: 那玩意儿谁看啊！
t_en: Who even looks at that!

t_ch: 啾，筑梦师小鸟也是这么说的！
t_en: Tweet, that's what the Dreamweaver bird said!

t_ch: 不管了，这份工作太累，罢工了，罢工了啾！
t_en: Forget it. This job is too tiring. I'm going on strike, going on a strike, tweet!

t_ch: 谢谢，但我有小地图。
t_en: Thank you, but I have a minimap.

t_ch: 啾…有地图，还逼我在这儿指路！
t_en: Tweet... There's a map, but I'm still forced to give directions here!

t_ch: 罢工了，罢工了啾！
t_en: Going on a strike, going on a strike, tweet!

t_ch: 祝你生日快乐啾~
t_en: Happy birthday to you, tweet!

t_ch: 祝你生日快乐啾啾~
t_en: Happy birthday to you, tweet tweet!

t_ch: 祝你…你叫什么来着啾？
t_en: Happy... what's your name, tweet?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 祝{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}生日快乐啾啾啾~
t_en: Happy birthday to you, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, tweet tweet tweet!

t_ch: 银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm Galactic Baseballer.
